title: Co-cultivation protocol for transforming wheat stripe rust
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fale7","text":"Protocol modified for Puccinia striiformis f. sp tritici (Pst), based on:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":22,"length":20},{"style":"italic","offset":49,"length":7},{"style":"italic","offset":58,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7tqus","text":"Michielse, C. B., Hooykaas, P. J., van den Hondel, C. A., \u0026 Ram, A. F. (2008). Agrobacterium-mediated transformation of the filamentous fungus Aspergillus awamori. Nature protocols, 3(10), 1671-1678. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":164,"length":16},{"style":"italic","offset":182,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8s7qm","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"td61","text":"I am grateful for critical suggestions from Prof. John Rathjen and Dr. Benjamin Schwessinger.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"592d8","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"9pj6s","text":"Chemical solutions:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c8nhk","text":"All chemicals used were from Sigma-Aldrich of the highest obtainable grades.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3lpg4","text":"Buffers are best when fresh and not older than 3-6 months","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"394bj","text":"0.2 M Acetosyringone (AS): Dissolve 785 mg of AS in DMSO to make up a total volume of 20 ml; aliquot and store in the dark at -20 oC. Do not thaw and use an aliquot more than twice, as activity of AS decreases during thawing and freezing","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":130,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"11rh1","text":"1% CaCl2 (wt/vol): Dissolve 10 g of CaCl2•2H2O in water to make up a total volume of 1 litter; autoclave.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":7,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":40,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":44,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1r7lp","text":"0.01% FeSO4 (wt/vol): Dissolve 0.1 g of FeSO4•7H2O in water to make up a total volume of 1 liter; filter-sterilize.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":10,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":44,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":48,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"pti6","text":"20% glucose (wt/vol): Dissolve 200 g of glucose in water to make up a total volume of 1 litter; autoclave.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9p2f5","text":"50% glycerol: Add 50 ml of glycerol to 50 ml of water to make up a total volume of 100 ml; autoclave.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bgd40","text":"1.25 M KH2PO4: Dissolve 170.1 g of KH2PO4 in water to make up a total volume of 1 litter; autoclave.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":9,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":12,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":37,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":40,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"274v0","text":"1.25 M K2HPO4: Dissolve 217.7 g of K2HPO4 in water to make up a total volume of 1 litter; autoclave.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":8,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":12,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":36,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":40,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"72dop","text":"1 M MES: Dissolve 195.24 g of MES in water to make up a total volume of 1 litter; adjust to pH 5.5 by adding NaOH; filter-sterilize. Solution can be stored for a month in the dark or alternatively aliquot and frozen at -20oC.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":222,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c0lit","text":"K-buffer: Add solution 1.25 M KH2PO4 to 1.25 M K2HPO4 until pH 4.8 is reached.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":32,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":35,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":48,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":52,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fkg79","text":"MN buffer: Dissolve 30 g of MgSO4•7H2O and 15 g of NaCl in water to make up a total volume of 1 litter; autoclave.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":32,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bk1tr","text":"20% NH4NO3 (wt/vol): Dissolve 200 g of NH4NO3 in water to make up a total volume of 1 litter; autoclave.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":6,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":9,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":41,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":44,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fhav4","text":"Trace elements for IM medium: Dissolve 100 mg of ZnSO4•7H2O, 100 mg of CuSO4•5H2O, 100 mg of H3BO3, 100 mg of MnSO4•H2O and 100 mg of Na2MoO4•2H2O in water to make up a total volume of 1 litter; autoclave.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":53,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":57,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":75,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":79,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":94,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":97,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":114,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":117,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":136,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":140,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":144,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"99cg5","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8hk0j","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4im3","text":"Reagent set up:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7901j","text":"Hybond N+ filter (Amersham) need to be cut into small stripes at 5 mm in width. Do not autoclave.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ro26","text":"Induction medium (IM) (liquid): Add 0.8 ml of K-buffer, 20 ml of MN buffer, 1 ml of 1% (wt/vol) CaCl2•2H2O, 10 ml of 0.01% (wt/vol) FeSO4, 5 ml of trace elements for IM medium, 2.5 ml of 20% (wt/vol) NH4NO3, 10 ml of 50% (vol/vol) glycerol, 40 ml of 1 M MES, pH 5.5, and 10 ml of 20% (wt/vol) glucose to 900.7 ml of sterilized water to make up 1 litter of liquid IM. No glucose IM just substitute 10 ml 20% glucose by 10 ml H2O.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":100,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":104,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":136,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":202,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":205,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":425,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3c8d9","text":"IM (solid): Dissolve 15 g of bacto agar in water to make up a total volume of 905.7 ml; autoclave. Add 0.8 ml of K-buffer, 20 ml of MN buffer, 1 ml of 1% (wt/vol) CaCl2•2H2O, 10 ml of 0.01% (wt/vol) FeSO4, 5 ml of trace elements for IM medium, 2.5 ml of 20% (wt/vol) NH4NO3, 10 ml of 50% (vol/vol) glycerol, 40 ml of 1 M MES, pH 5.5, and 5 ml of 20% (wt/vol) glucose to make up 1 litter of solid IM.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":167,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":171,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":203,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":269,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":272,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6n7es","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1odb3","text":"Wheat cultivar used: Morocco","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":21,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3dlgm","text":"A. tumefaciens strain: GV3101 pM90","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f3bkf","text":"Pst strain used: P. striiformis f.sp. tritici strain 104E137A-","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":3},{"style":"italic","offset":17,"length":15},{"style":"italic","offset":38,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"avop0","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c97ga","text":"Pst spore reproduction and collection:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":38},{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3ffi","text":"Spores are resuspended in 3MTM NovecTM 7100 engineered fluids immediately before inoculation. 7-day old seedlings were inoculated with fresh urediniospores of Pst using flat brush and incubated for 24 h in 100% humidity at 8oC in the dark. Subsequently plants were transferred to a growth chamber at 17oC with a 16:8 light cycle. Spores can be harvested after 14 dai, with 2 days intervals, by dusting the heavily infected leaves on aluminium foil or a weighing paper.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":28,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":36,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":224,"length":1},{"style":"sup","offset":302,"length":1},{"style":"italic","offset":159,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b5rqr","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"chvo1","text":"GUS staining of Pst spores:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":27},{"style":"italic","offset":16,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ffmi1","text":"substrate sollution: 0.05M sodium phosphate buffer, pH7.4; 1mM EDTA; 0.1% (v/v) TritonX-100; 0.1mM K3Fe(CN)6; 0.5mg X-Gluc/ml","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":100,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":107,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b7lsu","text":"Freshly harvested Pst spores are germinated on the surface of water for 6 h, before replacing all water by the substrate sollution using glass Pasteur pipette. Spores in the substrate sollution are then incubated at 37 oC for 40 h, before moving to a glass slice using glass Pasteur pipette for imaging.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":18,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bdg25","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9tk2d","text":"DNA extraction from Pst spores is according to Yiheng Hu: High quality DNA extraction from Fungi_small scale. protocols.io https://www.protocols.io/view/high-quality-dna-extraction-from-fungi-small-scale-exmbfk6","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":30},{"style":"italic","offset":20,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"eqime","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8skt3","text":"Critical steps to obtain high transformation efficiency:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":56}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"39l62","text":"Must use fresh spores/wheat seedlings/Acetosyringone/Agrobacterium","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":53,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"frmi2","text":"Must use glass plates/beakers to germinate spores","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5n2gk","text":"Make sure the filter contain co-cultured spores attached tightly to the wheat leaf to maximize the infection.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"ahgtj","text":"Grow 12 pots of wheat seedlings (15 seeds/pot) and infect them with 50 uL of Pst spores to reproduce fresh spores for co-culture.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":77,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"47qar","text":"Plant certain numbers of wheat (depend on each experiment, I do 6 to 8 pots) 7 days before the experiment start (day 1)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7urtt","text":"Streak out A. tumefaciens contains the T-DNA in binary vector onto a LB-Agar plate and grow at 28oC for 48 h. Pick up one colony of the A. tumefaciens into 5 ml liquid LB media with proper selection and grow overnight. The co-culture experiment need to start from a nice overnight A. tumefaciens culture.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":11,"length":14},{"style":"italic","offset":136,"length":14},{"style":"italic","offset":281,"length":14},{"style":"sup","offset":97,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Centrifuge the overnight A. tumefaciens culture at 5000xg for 10 mins, wash the pellet using induction media (IM, with glucose) once and resuspend the pellet with 5 mL IM (with glucose). 00:10:00
Add 2ml of Agro-IM solution into 8ml liquid IM (with glucose) in a 50 mL falcon tube and put it at 28 o C, shake until OD 600 =0.6 (~6 h depends on Agrobacterium strain )

Harvest fresh spores from previous infected wheat, estimate the number of spores based on a counting experiment: I first suspended them 0.1% (v/v) of the non-ionic detergent Triton X-100 to wet and separate them. Then I diluted the spore solution 10, 100 and 1000 times, and counted Pst spores using a haemocytometer I also estimated the spore volume by comparing the volume of a known number of spores with equivalent volumes of water in an Eppendorf tube. I estimated that 10 mg (~20 ml) of Pst spores equals 3.5 x 10 6 spores.

Dust 100ul of spores onto a glass plate/beaker at 14 cm in diameter (indicated by the image below), gently spray water on to the surface using a water sprayer to help the spores distribute equally. Cover the plate using parafilm and put it into 8 o C for ~6 h a

Take out four IM-Agar plates (with 200uM AS added) and take out the spores from 8 o C. Using sterilized tweezers to tweeze the cut hybond N+ filter to attach spores from the surface. Dry the filter a little using the airflow in the hood for 1min, and put it onto IM-Agar (spore side up). Normally five pieces of filter strips per plate and two plates per pot of wheat seedlings (~ 15 seedlings/pot). a
Pipette 100ul IM (no glucose, AS added) containing A. tumefaciens onto each filter with spores, wait them to dry for 3mins and put them at 20 o C to process the co-culture. a 36:00:00

After the IM solution containing A. tumefaciens reaches OD=0.6, centrifuge at room temperature at 5000xg for 10mins, resuspend it using 10 ml liquid IM (without glucose) with 200 uM Acetosyringone (AS) added.

After co-cultivation of 36 h, take out the plates from the constant temperature room and prepare previously grown 6-8 pots of fresh 9-day old wheat seedlings. Tweeze the filter up from plate, gently spray water onto it and attach onto the wheat leaf (primary leaf). After all filter is attached, gently spray a little water onto the plants and then put them into 100% humidity (a container with water) at 8 o C to germinate 24 h again. a 24:00:00
Transfer the infected wheat into growth chamber (17 o C 16:8 light:dark cycle, 60% humidity).
After 8 days, check the infection of spores on the wheat, and take off the filter papers to let pustules erupt. Plant 8 pots of wheat to amplify the transformed spores for selection
Collect spores, reinfect a small number of spores for amplification and further analysis (GUS staining, DNA extraction etc.).
creator: Yiheng Hu
